```asp
    input(R+1, C, Color),

    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color), input(R, C+2, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color), input(R+1, C+1, Color), input(R+1, C+2, Color),
    input(R+2, C, Color), input(R+2, C+1, Color), input(R+2, C+2, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+3, Color); input(R+3, C, Color)).

    object_2x2(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

    object_3x3(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

% All 4s in input
four_in_input(R, C) :- input(R, C, 4).

% Check if (R,C) is in any 2x2 block
not_in_2x2(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_2x2(R, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C-1, 4).

% Check if (R,C) is in any 3x3 block
not_in_3x3(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_3x3(R, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-2, 4).

% Isolated 4s: not in any block
isolated_4(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not_in_2x2(R, C),
    not_in_3x3(R, C).

% === Step 3: Determine output grid bounds from expanded 5x5 blocks and isolated 4s ===
% For each block center, expand to 5x5: from (R-2, C-2) to (R+2, C+2)
min_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R - 2, R = R1.
max_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R + 2, R = R1.
min_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C - 2, C = C1.
max_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C + 2, C = C1.

% For isolated 4s
min_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
max_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
min_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.
max_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.

% Final output bounds: min and max across all sources
min_row_final(R) :- min_row_exp(R).
min_col_final(C) :- min_col_exp(C).
max_row_final(R) :- max_row_exp(R).
max_col_final(C) :- max_col_exp(C).

% Also include bounds from isolated 4s
min_row_final(R) :- min_row_4(R).
min_col_final(C) :- min_col_4(C).
max_row_final(R) :- max_row_4(R).
max_col_final(C) :- max_col_4(C).

% Compute actual min/max values
min_row(R) :- min_row_final(R), #min{R : min_row_final(R)} = R.
max_row(R) :- max_row_final(R), #max{R : max_row_final(R)} = R.
min_col(C) :- min_col_final(C), #min{C : min_col_final(C)} = C.
max_col(C) :- max_col_final(C), #max{C : max_col_final(C)} = C.

% Generate all output cell coordinates
output_cell(R, C) :-
    min_row(MR),
    max_row(MR2),
    min_col(MC),
    max_col(MC2),
    R = MR + I,
    I >= 0,
    I <= MR2 - MR,
    C = MC + J,
    J >= 0,
    J <= MC2 - MC.

% 1. All 5x5 expanded blocks must be filled with the original block color
% For each block center (R, C) with color, the 5x5 area around it must be filled with that color

   C1 = C - 2,
   C2 = C + 2,

   R0 <= R2,
   C0 = C1,
   C0 <= C2,

% 3. No extra 4s in output

% 4. Background (0) must be used for all non-expanded and non-4 cells
```

```asp
% === Colors (hardcoded) ===
color(0..9).

% === Step 1: Identify maximal 2x2 and 3x3 blocks of non-zero color ===
% 2x2 blocks
object_2x2(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color),
    input(R+1, C+1, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+2, Color); input(R+2, C, Color)).

% 3x3 blocks
object_3x3(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color), input(R, C+2, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color), input(R+1, C+1, Color), input(R+1, C+2, Color),
    input(R+2, C, Color), input(R+2, C+1, Color), input(R+2, C+2, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+3, Color); input(R+3, C, Color)).

% === Step 2: Compute centers of blocks (R+1, C+1) ===
block_center(R, C, Color) :-
    object_2x2(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

block_center(R, C, Color) :-
    object_3x3(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

% === Step 3: Identify isolated 4s (not part of any block) ===
% All 4s in input
four_in_input(R, C) :- input(R, C, 4).

% A 4 is not part of any 2x2 block if it's not in any 2x2 block
not_in_2x2(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_2x2(R, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C-1, 4).

% A 4 is not part of any 3x3 block if it's not in any 3x3 block
not_in_3x3(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_3x3(R, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-2, 4).

% Isolated 4s: not in any block
isolated_4(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not_in_2x2(R, C),
    not_in_3x3(R, C).

% === Step 4: Determine output grid bounds ===
% Expand each block center to 5x5: (R-2, C-2) to (R+2, C+2)
min_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R - 2, R = R1.
max_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R + 2, R = R1.
min_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C - 2, C = C1.
max_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C + 2, C = C1.

% Include bounds from isolated 4s
min_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
max_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
min_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.
max_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.

% Final min/max across all sources
min_row(R) :- min_row_exp(R), #min{R : min_row_exp(R)} = R.
max_row(R) :- max_row_exp(R), #max{R : max_row_exp(R)} = R.
min_col(C) :- min_col_exp(C), #min{C : min_col_exp(C)} = C.
max_col(C) :- max_col_exp(C), #max{C : max_col_exp(C)} = C.

% Also include 4s bounds
min_row(R) :- min_row_4(R), #min{R : min_row_4(R)} = R.
max_row(R) :- max_row_4(R), #max{R : max_row_4(R)} = R.
min_col(C) :- min_col_4(C), #min{C : min_col_4(C)} = C.
max_col(C) :- max_col_4(C), #max{C : max_col_4(C)} = C.

% Generate all output cell coordinates
output_cell(R, C) :-
    min_row(MR),
    max_row(MR2),
    min_col(MC),
    max_col(MC2),
    R = MR + I,
    I >= 0,
    I <= MR2 - MR,
    C = MC + J,
    J >= 0,
    J <= MC2 - MC.

% === Step 5: Assign colors to output cells ===
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% === Step 6: Integrity constraints ===
% 1. All 5x5 expanded blocks must be filled with the original block color
:- block_center(R, C, Color),
   R1 = R - 2,
   R2 = R + 2,
   C1 = C - 2,
   C2 = C + 2,
   R0 = R1,
   R0 <= R2,
   C0 = C1,
   C0 <= C2,
   not output(R0, C0, Color).

% 2. Isolated 4s must be preserved in output
:- isolated_4(R, C), output_cell(R, C), not output(R, C, 4).

% 3. No extra 4s in output
:- output(R, C, 4), not isolated_4(R, C), output_cell(R, C).

% 4. Non-expanded and non-4 cells must be 0
% If a cell has non-zero color, it must be either in a 5x5 block or a preserved 4
:- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0,
   not ( 
       #count{Rc, Cc : block_center(Rc, Cc, Color), abs(R - Rc) <= 2, abs(C - Cc) <= 2} > 0
   ),
   not isolated_4(R, C).
```

```asp
% === Colors (hardcoded) ===
color(0..9).

% === Step 1: Identify maximal 2x2 and 3x3 blocks of non-zero color ===
% 2x2 blocks
object_2x2(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color),
    input(R+1, C+1, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+2, Color); input(R+2, C, Color)).

% 3x3 blocks
object_3x3(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color), input(R, C+2, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color), input(R+1, C+1, Color), input(R+1, C+2, Color),
    input(R+2, C, Color), input(R+2, C+1, Color), input(R+2, C+2, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+3, Color); input(R+3, C, Color)).

% === Step 2: Compute centers of blocks (R+1, C+1) ===
block_center(R, C, Color) :-
    object_2x2(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

block_center(R, C, Color) :-
    object_3x3(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

% === Step 3: Identify isolated 4s (not part of any block) ===
% All 4s in input
four_in_input(R, C) :- input(R, C, 4).

% A 4 is not part of any 2x2 block
not_in_2x2(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_2x2(R, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C-1, 4).

% A 4 is not part of any 3x3 block
not_in_3x3(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_3x3(R, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-2, 4).

% Isolated 4s: not in any block
isolated_4(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not_in_2x2(R, C),
    not_in_3x3(R, C).

% === Step 4: Determine output grid bounds ===
% Expand each block center to 5x5: (R-2, C-2) to (R+2, C+2)
min_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R - 2, R = R1.
max_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R + 2, R = R1.
min_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C - 2, C = C1.
max_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C + 2, C = C1.

% Include bounds from isolated 4s
min_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
max_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
min_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.
max_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.

% Final min/max across all sources
min_row(R) :- min_row_exp(R), #min{R : min_row_exp(R)} = R.
max_row(R) :- max_row_exp(R), #max{R : max_row_exp(R)} = R.
min_col(C) :- min_col_exp(C), #min{C : min_col_exp(C)} = C.
max_col(C) :- max_col_exp(C), #max{C : max_col_exp(C)} = C.

% Also include 4s bounds
min_row(R) :- min_row_4(R), #min{R : min_row_4(R)} = R.
max_row(R) :- max_row_4(R), #max{R : max_row_4(R)} = R.
min_col(C) :- min_col_4(C), #min{C : min_col_4(C)} = C.
max_col(C) :- max_col_4(C), #max{C : max_col_4(C)} = C.

% Generate all output cell coordinates
output_cell(R, C) :-
    min_row(MR),
    max_row(MR2),
    min_col(MC),
    max_col(MC2),
    R = MR + I,
    I >= 0,
    I <= MR2 - MR,
    C = MC + J,
    J >= 0,
    J <= MC2 - MC.

% === Step 5: Assign colors to output cells ===
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% === Step 6: Integrity constraints ===
% 1. All 5x5 expanded blocks must be filled with the original block color
:- block_center(R, C, Color),
   R1 = R - 2,
   R2 = R + 2,
   C1 = C - 2,
   C2 = C + 2,
   R0 = R1,
   R0 <= R2,
   C0 = C1,
   C0 <= C2,
   not output(R0, C0, Color).

% 2. Isolated 4s must be preserved in output
:- isolated_4(R, C), output_cell(R, C), not output(R, C, 4).

% 3. No extra 4s in output
:- output(R, C, 4), not isolated_4(R, C), output_cell(R, C).

% 4. Non-expanded and non-4 cells must be 0

       #count{Rc, Cc : block_center(Rc, Cc, Color), abs(R - Rc) <= 2, abs(C - Cc) <= 2} > 0
   ),
```

```asp
% === Colors (hardcoded) ===
color(0..9).

% === Step 1: Identify maximal 2x2 and 3x3 blocks of non-zero color ===
% 2x2 blocks
object_2x2(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color),
    input(R+1, C+1, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+2, Color); input(R+2, C, Color)).

% 3x3 blocks
object_3x3(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color), input(R, C+2, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color), input(R+1, C+1, Color), input(R+1, C+2, Color),
    input(R+2, C, Color), input(R+2, C+1, Color), input(R+2, C+2, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+3, Color); input(R+3, C, Color)).

% === Step 2: Compute centers of blocks (R+1, C+1) ===
block_center(R, C, Color) :-
    object_2x2(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

block_center(R, C, Color) :-
    object_3x3(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

% === Step 3: Identify isolated 4s (not part of any block) ===
% All 4s in input
four_in_input(R, C) :- input(R, C, 4).

% A 4 is not part of any 2x2 block
not_in_2x2(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_2x2(R, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C-1, 4).

% A 4 is not part of any 3x3 block
not_in_3x3(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_3x3(R, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-2, 4).

% Isolated 4s: not in any block
isolated_4(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not_in_2x2(R, C),
    not_in_3x3(R, C).

% === Step 4: Determine output grid bounds ===
% Expand each block center to 5x5: (R-2, C-2) to (R+2, C+2)
min_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R - 2, R = R1.
max_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R + 2, R = R1.
min_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C - 2, C = C1.
max_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C + 2, C = C1.

% Include bounds from isolated 4s
min_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
max_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
min_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.
max_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.
```

```asp
% === Colors (hardcoded) ===
color(0..9).

% === Step 1: Identify maximal 2x2 and 3x3 blocks of non-zero color ===
% 2x2 blocks
object_2x2(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color),
    input(R+1, C+1, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+2, Color); input(R+2, C, Color)).

% 3x3 blocks
object_3x3(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    input(R, C+1, Color), input(R, C+2, Color),
    input(R+1, C, Color), input(R+1, C+1, Color), input(R+1, C+2, Color),
    input(R+2, C, Color), input(R+2, C+1, Color), input(R+2, C+2, Color),
    % Maximal: cannot extend right or down
    not (input(R, C+3, Color); input(R+3, C, Color)).

% === Step 2: Compute centers of blocks (R+1, C+1) ===
block_center(R, C, Color) :-
    object_2x2(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

block_center(R, C, Color) :-
    object_3x3(R, C, Color),
    R1 = R + 1,
    C1 = C + 1,
    R = R1,
    C = C1.

% === Step 3: Identify isolated 4s (not part of any block) ===
% All 4s in input
four_in_input(R, C) :- input(R, C, 4).

% A 4 is not part of any 2x2 block
not_in_2x2(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_2x2(R, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_2x2(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_2x2(R-1, C-1, 4).

% A 4 is not part of any 3x3 block
not_in_3x3(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not object_3x3(R, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C, 4),
    not object_3x3(R, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-1, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-1, C-2, 4),
    not object_3x3(R-2, C-2, 4).

% Isolated 4s: not in any block
isolated_4(R, C) :-
    four_in_input(R, C),
    not_in_2x2(R, C),
    not_in_3x3(R, C).

% === Step 4: Determine output grid bounds ===
% Expand each block center to 5x5: (R-2, C-2) to (R+2, C+2)
min_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R - 2, R = R1.
max_row_exp(R) :- block_center(R, _, _), R1 = R + 2, R = R1.
min_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C - 2, C = C1.
max_col_exp(C) :- block_center(_, C, _), C1 = C + 2, C = C1.

% Include bounds from isolated 4s
min_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
max_row_4(R) :- isolated_4(R, _), R = R.
min_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.
max_col_4(C) :- isolated_4(_, C), C = C.

% Final min/max across all sources
min_row(R) :- min_row_exp(R), #min{R : min_row_exp(R)} = R.
max_row(R) :- max_row_exp(R), #max{R : max_row_exp(R)} = R.
min_col(C) :- min_col_exp(C), #min{C : min_col_exp(C)} = C.
max_col(C) :- max_col_exp(C), #max{C : max_col_exp(C)} = C.

% Also include 4s bounds
min_row(R) :- min_row_4(R), #min{R : min_row_4(R)} = R.
max_row(R) :- max_row_4(R), #max{R : max_row_4(R)} = R.
min_col(C) :- min_col_4(C), #min{C : min_col_4(C)} = C.
max_col(C) :- max_col_4(C), #max{C : max_col_4(C)} = C.

% Generate all output cell coordinates
output_cell(R, C) :-
    min_row(MR),
    max_row(MR2),
    min_col(MC),
    max_col(MC2),
    R = MR + I,
    I >= 0,
    I <= MR2 - MR,
    C = MC + J,
    J >= 0,
    J <= MC2 - MC.

% === Step 5: Assign colors to output cells ===
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% === Step 6: Integrity constraints ===
% 1. All 5x5 expanded blocks must be filled with the original block color
:- block_center(R, C, Color),
   R1 = R - 2,
   R2 = R + 2,
   C1 = C - 2,
   C2 = C + 2,
   R0 = R1,
   R0 <= R2,
   C0 = C1,
   C0 <= C2,
   not output(R0, C0, Color).

% 2. Isolated 4s must be preserved in output
:- isolated_4(R, C), output_cell(R, C), not output(R, C, 4).

% 3. No extra 4s in output
:- output(R, C, 4), not isolated_4(R, C), output_cell(R, C).

% 4. Non-expanded and non-4 cells must be 0
% If a cell has non-zero color, it must be either in a 5x5 block or a preserved 4
in_5x5_block(R, C, Color) :-
    block_center(Rc, Cc, Color),
    abs(R - Rc) <= 2,
    abs(C - Cc) <= 2.

:- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0,
   not in_5x5_block(R, C, Color),
   not isolated_4(R, C).
```
